title: Split doctor into a module and add TTY-aware prompt primitives
action: Split src/doctor.rs into a src/doctor/ module: mod.rs (run() orchestration), checks.rs (the 10 existing checks), prompt.rs (new). The 10 checks today are inline blocks inside run() (src/doctor.rs:49-213), not separate functions - move the Check struct and its construction logic into checks.rs as free functions (one per check, or a builder returning Vec<Check>) that mod.rs's run() calls in the same order, rather than leaving them as inline blocks; do not change any check's logic or output text. In prompt.rs add: (a) TTY detection per D15 that requires BOTH stdin and stderr to be a terminal, via std::io::IsTerminal from the standard library; (b) prompt primitives that write their prompt text to STDERR, never stdout, so a piped stdout still yields a clean report — yes/no confirm with default, line input with default, typed-confirmation, choose-from-numbered-list; (c) masked secret entry per D13 using the rpassword crate, which returns the value plus a non-revealing display form: length, plus the last 3 characters only when the value is at least 12 characters long, and length alone when shorter. Empty input must be representable as 'skipped'. Update tests/rename_contract.sh's source-surface inventory so the new module paths replace src/doctor.rs. This cell changes NO observable behavior: doctor's report, its check order, and its exit code stay exactly as today per D5, and every primitive here is unused until Slice 2 wires it.

truths: doctor's printed report and exit code are byte-identical to before the split for the same environment.
TTY detection reports interactive only when stdin AND stderr are both terminals, per D15.
Prompt text is written to stderr, never stdout, so a redirected stdout contains only the report.
The masked-entry display form never contains the full secret: it shows length, plus the last 3 characters only when the value is at least 12 characters, and never the leading characters.
Empty secret input is representable as an explicit skip rather than an empty secret.
rpassword is the only dependency added, and TTY detection itself adds none.
Prompt and display-form logic are unit-tested without requiring a real terminal.
migrate_default_state_if is still never invoked in doctor mode, per D12.

artifacts.substantive: dual-TTY detection, stderr-directed confirm/line/typed-confirm/select primitives, and rpassword-backed masked entry with a non-revealing display form, all unit-tested
the 10 existing checks moved without logic changes
source-surface inventory updated from src/doctor.rs to the new module paths
rpassword added as the single new dependency

key_links: src/lib.rs declares the doctor module; src/main.rs continues to call doctor::run() unchanged
prohibitions: Do not add any prompt or TTY framework (dialoguer, inquire, requestty, crossterm, atty, is-terminal); rpassword for masked entry is the only permitted new dependency.
Do not hand-roll terminal echo suppression via termios or the Windows Console API.
Do not include a full secret value in any display form, log line, or error message.
Do not wire any prompt into any check in this cell.
Do not change any check's logic, order, or output text, and do not remove the socket-check early return here; that is D14 work for a later slice.
Do not add the --check flag in this cell; it has no meaning until interactive mode exists.
Do not make doctor write, create, or mutate any file.

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash tests/rename_contract.sh

trace.outcome: Split src/doctor.rs into src/doctor/{mod.rs,checks.rs,prompt.rs}: mod.rs keeps run() orchestration and print_report unchanged; checks.rs holds the 10 existing checks as a build_checks() builder with zero logic/output changes; prompt.rs adds dual-TTY detection (stdin+stderr) and stderr-directed confirm/line/typed-confirm/choose primitives plus rpassword-backed masked secret entry with a non-revealing display form (length, +last-3-chars only at >=12 chars). Nothing wired into checks yet. Updated tests/rename_contract.sh source-surface inventory to the new module paths. Added rpassword as the sole new dependency.
trace.verify_output: cargo test: 124 passed (0 failed); cargo clippy -D warnings: silent; tests/rename_contract.sh: 'rename contract: ok' exit 0 (3 rg-not-found lines are a pre-existing sandbox gap, harmless since those checks are inside if-guards and the baseline run before any change showed the same lines with exit 0)
trace.verify_command: cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash tests/rename_contract.sh
